rounds.agent_message: Seed round copied from the highest existing source round.

Implemented the round-1 kernel in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p82/optimization_rounds/round1/output_model_new.py) as an MFMA-based fixed-shape asym Conv2D path with the required `128x128` block tile, fixed `2x2` warp grid, fixed `32x32x8` BF16 MFMA fragment mapping, and the specified accumulator/writeback layout. The optimized path is cudagraph-safe: `forward()` reuses a cached device weight tensor via `_get_cached_weight()` and only rebuilds it when the weight storage pointer changes. I also aligned the harnesses in [test_conv2d_asym.py](/workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py) and [bench_conv2d.py](/workspace/substrate/benchmark/conv2d/bench_conv2d.py) to this round’s supported configuration, and wrote [case.txt](/workspace/kernel_benchmark/path/to/case.txt) with the required round-directory path. Validation passed: ```text python -m pytest /workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py 2 passed in 3.38s ```

Implemented the split-K MFMA conv2d path in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p82/optimization_rounds/round2/output_model_new.py), with `SPLIT_K_SLICES = 2`, fp32 atomic reduction into a cached workspace, and a separate finalize kernel that writes bf16 NCHW output. The forward path now reuses cached weight/workspace/output buffers to avoid rebuilding device-side state during capture. I also updated the round-specific callsites in [test_conv2d_asym.py](/workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py) and [bench_conv2d.py](/workspace/substrate/benchmark/conv2d/bench_conv2d.py), and wrote [case.txt](/workspace/kernel_benchmark/path/to/case.txt). Validation passed: ```bash python -m pytest /workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py ``` `2 passed in 3.32s`
